channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Aid worker says hundreds remain trapped after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: After earthquakes shattered much of the capital in Venezuela, Project Hope's Cesar Jimenez says rescue crews are racing to save hundreds trapped as international aid arrives and hospitals struggle to cope.
item.content:encoded:
After earthquakes shattered much of the capital in Venezuela, Project Hope's Cesar Jimenez says rescue crews are racing to save hundreds trapped as international aid arrives and hospitals struggle to cope.

(Image credit: Javier Campos)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: 'We are with you, Venezuela': Houston community rallies after deadly earthquakes
item.description: Venezuelans living in the U.S organize donation drives in response to the devastating earthquakes.
item.content:encoded:
Venezuelans living in the U.S organize donation drives in response to the devastating earthquakes.

(Image credit: Lucio Vasquez)
item.dc:creator: Lucio Vasquez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Rescuers in Venezuela continue search for the missing after devastating earthquakes, SCOTUS rulings give Trump more power to set immigration policy, Trump works to woo struggling American farmers.
item.content:encoded: Rescuers in Venezuela continue search for the missing after devastating earthquakes, SCOTUS rulings give Trump more power to set immigration policy, Trump works to woo struggling American farmers.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Olivia Wilde talks about her new film, 'The Invite'
item.description: In the new comedy "The Invite," a couple on the brink of splitting up throws a dinner party for a mysterious couple living nearby. NPR's A Martinez speaks with actor and director Olivia Wilde.
item.content:encoded: In the new comedy "The Invite," a couple on the brink of splitting up throws a dinner party for a mysterious couple living nearby. NPR's A Martinez speaks with actor and director Olivia Wilde.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Organizers say the Great American State Fair is for everyone. Some disagree
item.description: The 16-day state fair on the National Mall kicked off with a Trump rally, and not all 50 states are taking part. Organizers say there's nothing partisan about celebrating America's 250th birthday.
item.content:encoded:
The 16-day state fair on the National Mall kicked off with a Trump rally, and not all 50 states are taking part. Organizers say there's nothing partisan about celebrating America's 250th birthday.

(Image credit: Andrew Leyden)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: In a first since Trump deployed the guard to D.C., Democrats are sending troops
item.description: Several Democratic governors have sent National Guard troops to Washington, D.C., ahead of America 250, amid President Trump's highly visible — and controversial — ongoing deployment to the city.
item.content:encoded:
Several Democratic governors have sent National Guard troops to Washington, D.C., ahead of America 250, amid President Trump's highly visible — and controversial — ongoing deployment to the city.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: HBCUs use World Cup excitement to grow support for their soccer teams
item.description: Excitement over the World Cup is giving soccer players at Historically Black Colleges and Universities a chance to highlight their teams and gain support.
item.content:encoded: Excitement over the World Cup is giving soccer players at Historically Black Colleges and Universities a chance to highlight their teams and gain support.
item.dc:creator: Leoneda Inge

item.title: Problem Solvers Caucus co-chairs say they'll make sure housing bill becomes law
item.description: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., and Rep. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., tell NPR's Michel Martin they want to force into law a housing bill President Trump has refused to sign.
item.content:encoded: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., and Rep. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., tell NPR's Michel Martin they want to force into law a housing bill President Trump has refused to sign.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: SCOTUS rulings gives Trump more power to set immigration policy
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court gives President Trump more power to set immigration policy with a pair of decisions Thursday.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Supreme Court gives President Trump more power to set immigration policy with a pair of decisions Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Ohio pastor shares how the SCOTUS ruling on TPS holders will affect his community
item.description: The Supreme Court is letting President Trump remove protections from thousands of Haitian and Syrian refugees. Michel Martin asks a pastor in Springfield, Ohio, about his city's Haitian community.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court is letting President Trump remove protections from thousands of Haitian and Syrian refugees. Michel Martin asks a pastor in Springfield, Ohio, about his city's Haitian community.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
